title: tilocarpus papuanus
intro: Tilocarpus papuanus, also known as the Papuan Calabar bean or Tilocarpus bean, is a plant species native to Papua New Guinea, belonging to the family Apocynaceae. The plant contains the toxic alkaloid tilosine, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Tilocarpus papuanus include pain relief, wound healing, and treatment of fever and other infections. Tilocarpus papuanus can be prepared in various herbal forms, including extracts, tinctures, and infusions, although caution is advised due to its toxic properties and potential side effects.
